summary: Implemented most of ftCo_800C2600 afterimage rendering and completed ftCo_800C2FD8 in src/melee/ft/ftafterimage.c, with supporting type refinements for sword afterimage parameters in Mars and item headers. Bot report recorded ftCo_800C2FD8 reaching 100% and ftCo_800C2600 improving to 88.71%; no human review comments were present in the loaded slice.

key_files.role: Main decompilation target; replaced NOT_IMPLEMENTED in ftCo_800C2600 with afterimage vertex generation, GX state setup, fighter/item parameter selection, vector math, and draw submission; also changed ftCo_800C2FD8 sword-item handling to a switch and HSD_ASSERTREPORT.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_800C2600 NOT_IMPLEMENTED replaced by full function body
raw_diff: ftCo_800C2FD8 if/else item-kind check replaced with switch and HSD_ASSERTREPORT
text_corpus issue_comment decomp-dev[bot]: ftCo_800C2600 0.22% to 88.71%; ftCo_800C2FD8 98.99% to 100.00%

key_files.role: Recovered fields in struct SwordAttrs from an opaque 0x14-byte pad into two f32 fields, color/alpha byte fields, and residual padding.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: SwordAttrs pad_x0[0x14] replaced by x0, x4, x8-x10, pad_x11[3]

key_files.role: Refined struct itSword_UnkBytes so offsets +0 and +4 are f32 instead of individual u8 bytes, matching use as afterimage scale parameters.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itSword_UnkBytes x0/x4 changed from byte fields to f32 fields

what_changed: ftCo_800C2600 was implemented with early exits for arg1 != 2 and insufficient afterimage history, GX blend/alpha/Z/TEV/channel/cull setup, afterimage parameter selection from sword items or Link/Mars fighter attributes, path-distance accumulation, interpolated vertex generation, optional angular subdivision using lbVector_CrossprodNormalized/lbVector_Angle/lbVector_RotateAboutUnitAxis, GX triangle-strip emission, and HSD_StateInvalidate(-1).

ftCo_800C2FD8 was adjusted so sword item handling uses switch (itGetKind(...)) with It_Kind_Sword and default HSD_ASSERTREPORT(0x16D, 0, "no afterimage item!\n") instead of OSReport plus raw __assert.

Sword-related afterimage parameter types were refined: Mars SwordAttrs now exposes f32 x0, f32 x4, byte fields x8 through x10, and padding at x11; itSword_UnkBytes now exposes f32 x0 and f32 x4 before byte fields at x8 and beyond.
The file gained includes for GX, MTX, baselib debug/cobj/state, Link and Mars types, lbvector, math_ppc, plus a local AfterimageVtx struct and AFTERIMAGE_ANGLE_STEP constant.
The bot report recorded one new match, ftCo_800C2FD8 at 100.00%, and two improvements: ftCo_800C2600 to 88.71% and .sdata2 to 87.50%.
smart_moves: Recovered a small local vertex struct, AfterimageVtx, to express packed position and RGBA data used for GX emission instead of leaving raw buffer arithmetic.
Moved sword afterimage parameter accesses onto typed struct fields at offsets x0, x4, and x8-x10, supporting the implementation without raw byte offsets for those fields.
Used existing library/vector helpers and GX APIs directly, making the afterimage pipeline readable: lbVector_* helpers for interpolation and GX* calls for render-state and vertex submission.
Converted a raw OSReport plus __assert sequence in ftCo_800C2FD8 to HSD_ASSERTREPORT, consistent with the loaded assert/report macro standard when message/report behavior is represented.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a rendering function consumes structured parameter blobs, recovering just the fields proven by use can unlock readable code while leaving unknown tails or gaps as padding/address-style fields.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: itSword_UnkBytes x0/x4 changed to f32 and x8-x10 remain byte fields
raw_diff: SwordAttrs pad_x0[0x14] split into f32 and u8 fields with pad_x11[3]
raw_diff: ftCo_800C2600 uses params->x0, x4, x8, x9, xA, xB, xC, xE, xF, x10

decomp_lessons.lesson: For partially matched graphics code, a local vertex struct can clarify GXPosition/GXColor submission and intermediate vertex-buffer construction without inventing high-level semantic names for every unknown parameter.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: typedef struct AfterimageVtx { f32 x,y,z; u8 r,g,b,a; }
raw_diff: vtx_buf[152] populated then emitted via GXPosition3f32 and GXColor4u8

decomp_lessons.lesson: Assert/report cleanup can be part of a matching change when the macro preserves the file-line/message behavior visible in generated code.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: OSReport("no afterimage item!\n") and __assert("ftafterimage.c", 0x16D, "0") replaced by HSD_ASSERTREPORT(0x16D, 0, "no afterimage item!\n")

naming_conventions.name: AfterimageVtx
naming_conventions.evidence: Introduced in ftafterimage.c for position plus RGBA vertices used by afterimage GX drawing.

naming_conventions.name: AFTERIMAGE_ANGLE_STEP
naming_conventions.evidence: Introduced for the angle subdivision divisor 0.0872664600610733f in ftCo_800C2600.

naming_conventions.name: x0, x4, x8-x10
naming_conventions.evidence: The PR preserves offset-style names for recovered but still semantically unnamed fields in SwordAttrs and itSword_UnkBytes.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use nested scopes and tightly controlled local declarations to shape lifetimes in a large MWCC-matched function.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_800C2600 has multiple nested blocks introducing ringIdx, totalDist, curIdx2, distPtr, vp, and subdivision locals

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Express cyclic ring-buffer traversal explicitly with index wrap from 0 to 2 while iterating afterimage history.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: repeated curIdx/curIdx2 handling chooses idx - 1 or 2, and nextRingIdx wraps to 2

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Retain an unrolled-by-4 GX vertex emission loop with a remainder label/goto shape where the diff shows this was the chosen matching form.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: GX emission loop uses n4 = count >> 2, p[0]..p[3], count &= 3, goto remainder

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Switch form can replace an if/default item-kind check when it matches and leaves room for item-kind dispatch.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_800C2FD8 changed from if (itGetKind == It_Kind_Sword) else to switch with default

review_feedback.summary: GALE01 report showed matched code +0.01% (+436 bytes), one new match ftCo_800C2FD8 at 100.00%, ftCo_800C2600 improved to 88.71%, and .sdata2 improved to 87.50%.
No human review comments or review records were present in the loaded PR slice.

notes: The loaded diff is complete and supports changed-file and code-change extraction.
The PR body is empty and there are no human review comments, so reviewer intent and rationale are not available.

Bot report supports specific matching/improvement numbers for ftCo_800C2FD8, ftCo_800C2600, and .sdata2, but no local build command, objdiff invocation, or final regression ledger is included beyond that report.
Type field meanings remain mostly address-style; the PR evidence supports offsets and primitive types, not semantic names for each parameter.

The implementation contains matching-shaped control flow such as nested scopes and a goto remainder; evidence supports that these were present in the merged diff, but not that all alternatives were tested.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Typed afterimage parameter fields enabled readable ftafterimage code
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2407 recovered sword afterimage parameter blobs enough to use f32 fields at +0/+4 and byte color/alpha fields at +8 and beyond in ftCo_800C2600, replacing opaque padding while keeping conservative offset-style field names.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Assert/report macro cleanup coincided with ftafterimage match
accepted_candidate_records.text: In ftCo_800C2FD8, a raw OSReport plus __assert path for a missing afterimage item was replaced by HSD_ASSERTREPORT with the same line number and message context, and the bot report marked ftCo_800C2FD8 as 100.00%.

source_update_candidates.title: ftafterimage.c owns afterimage rendering implementation work for ftCo_800C2600 and ftCo_800C2FD8
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2407 changed src/melee/ft/ftafterimage.c, implementing ftCo_800C2600 afterimage rendering logic and completing ftCo_800C2FD8 according to the bot report.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path fact for future workers looking for fighter afterimage rendering and matching history.

source_update_candidates.title: Sword afterimage parameter fields appear in Mars and item type headers
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2407 refined struct SwordAttrs in src/melee/ft/chara/ftMars/types.h and struct itSword_UnkBytes in src/melee/it/items/types.h with f32 fields at +0/+4 and byte fields at later offsets used by ftCo_800C2600.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_800C2600 casts Link/Mars attrs to itSword_UnkBytes
source_update_candidates.reason: Could help future type-recovery work, but should be curated as source/path knowledge rather than treated as a global decomp standard.

source_update_candidates.title: Consider documenting assert/report macro cleanup with match evidence
source_update_candidates.text: This PR provides another example where OSReport plus raw __assert was represented as HSD_ASSERTREPORT while preserving the missing-afterimage-item message and line number and producing a bot-reported 100% function match for ftCo_800C2FD8.
source_update_candidates.reason: Existing assert-report-macros standard already covers this; curator may use as an example rather than a new rule.

rejection_notes: Do not infer semantic names for SwordAttrs or itSword_UnkBytes fields beyond the address-style names shown; the PR does not prove meanings such as inner radius, outer radius, or color role names.
Do not claim ftCo_800C2600 fully matched; the bot report only shows improvement to 88.71%.
Do not infer human reviewer approval rationale; no human review comments were loaded.
Do not promote the unrolled GX emission loop with goto remainder as a general preferred style; it is only evidenced as the shape used in this PR.
